name: ai-voice-cloning
description:
AI voice generation, text-to-speech, and voice synthesis via inference.sh CLI.
Models: Kokoro TTS, DIA, Chatterbox, Higgs, VibeVoice for natural speech.
Capabilities: multiple voices, emotions, accents, long-form narration, conversation.
Use for: voiceovers, audiobooks, podcasts, video narration, accessibility.
Triggers: voice cloning, tts, text to speech, ai voice, voice generation,
voice synthesis, voice over, narration, speech synthesis, ai narrator,
elevenlabs alternative, natural voice, realistic speech, voice ai
capabilities: Generate voiceovers using multiple TTS models including Kokoro, DIA, Chatterbox, Higgs, and VibeVoice
Select from 10+ voice options with different accents and speaking styles
Adjust speech speed and pacing with punctuation control
Create multi-voice conversations by generating separate audio tracks
Process long-form content in chunks for consistent audio quality
Merge audio files with crossfade effects for smooth transitions
author: inference-sh
securitySummary: No confirmed security issues found. All detected 'security findings' are documentation examples of CLI commands and legitimate URLs for the inference.sh service. The skill provides usage examples for an AI voice generation tool and contains no malicious code patterns. Usage examples are safe bash commands users would copy into their terminals.
valueStatement: Create natural-sounding AI voiceovers for videos, podcasts, and audiobooks using command-line tools. Generate professional voiceovers in seconds without recording equipment.
userTitle: Generate AI Voiceovers with One Command
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account
Generates audio files that need external media software for editing
Long text requires chunking to maintain quality
Internet connection needed for TTS service
Premium voice models may have usage limits

useCases.title: Content Creators
useCases.description: Add voiceovers to YouTube videos, podcasts, or social media content quickly without recording equipment.

useCases.title: Educators
useCases.description: Generate narrated course content and tutorials. Process long educational materials into audiobooks.

useCases.title: Business Teams
useCases.description: Create professional narration for company videos, earnings calls, and marketing materials.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Voiceover
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a voiceover for [text]. Use [voice] style. Keep the tone [professional/casual/expressive]. Output the infsh command to execute.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick voiceover generation for short content

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Speaker Dialogue
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a dialogue between two people discussing [topic]. Speaker A speaks [statement], Speaker B responds with [response]. Generate both audio files using different voices.

promptTemplates.title: Long-Form Narration
promptTemplates.prompt: Break this text into chunks of max 5000 characters for consistent voice quality: [text]. Generate each chunk with [voice], then merge with crossfade effects.

promptTemplates.title: Video Voiceover
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a voiceover for a video about [topic]. Use a professional voice and moderate speed (1.0). Generate the command to create audio for [video duration] seconds of content.

bestPractices: Use shorter sentences for more natural-sounding voiceovers
Match the voice selection to your content type and audience
Adjust speed to 0.9 for audiobooks and 1.1 for commercials
Add punctuation marks (periods, commas, ellipses) to control pacing
Process long content in 5000-character chunks for consistency
Use crossfade when merging multiple audio segments
antiPatterns: Using very long sentences without punctuation
Mixing multiple voices in a single command without merging
Generating voiceovers at inconsistent speeds across chunks
Using casual voices for professional business content
Attempting to clone voices with this tool (not supported)

faq.question: Do I need to record my own voice for voiceovers?
faq.answer: No. This tool uses AI to generate professional voiceovers. You can select from 10+ pre-trained voices with different accents and styles.

faq.question: What audio formats are supported?
faq.answer: The generated audio files can be used with any standard media player or video editing software. The service outputs audio in common formats compatible with most video editors.

faq.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the generated audio can be used in commercial content like videos, podcasts, and marketing materials. Check the inference.sh license terms for specific usage guidelines.

faq.question: How do I merge multiple audio files?
faq.answer: Use the media-merger app with crossfade settings. Add all generated audio files to the audio_files array and specify a crossfade duration in milliseconds.

faq.question: What's the maximum text length I can process?
faq.answer: The tool works best with content under 5000 characters per chunk. Longer text should be split into multiple segments for consistent voice quality.

faq.question: Which voice should I choose for professional content?
faq.answer: For professional business content, use male voices like 'am_michael' or 'am_echo' for authority, or female voices like 'af_nicole' for approachability. British accents (bf_emma, bm_george) work well for international audiences.
